skill.name: agile-ceremonies
skill.description: Guides agile teams through standups, retrospectives, sprint planning, progress tracking, team health checks, and action item documentation.
skill.summary: Run structured agile ceremonies with clear updates, risks, metrics, and follow-up actions.
skill.author: Joseph OBrien

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged Markdown backticks, agile progress language, and a YAML description line as dangerous patterns. Manual review found no executable scripts, shell commands, network behavior, weak cryptography, data exfiltration, or prompt injection attempts.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences
low_findings.description: Static analysis interpreted Markdown fenced template blocks as Ruby or shell backtick execution. These lines are documentation templates for standups and summaries, not executable commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are Markdown fence delimiters around sample ceremony templates. No command interpreter, shell syntax, variable expansion, or execution path is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Inline Markdown File Names
low_findings.description: Static analysis interpreted inline backticks around reference file names as external command execution. The lines only list documentation files used by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The backticks are Markdown inline-code formatting around relative file paths. They are not part of a script, command, or generated instruction to execute code.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Agile Status Language
low_findings.description: Static analysis labeled progress and blocker-related wording as network reconnaissance. The reviewed lines are sprint status headings, status labels, and quality checklist items.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is agile workflow reporting. There are no network utilities, host discovery commands, IP addresses, ports, URLs, or scanning instructions.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged the skill description line as a weak cryptographic algorithm. The line contains only the skill description and does not reference cryptographic primitives.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged line is YAML front matter describing agile ceremonies. No hash algorithm, encryption function, credential handling, or security-sensitive operation appears there.

content.user_title: Run Agile Ceremonies With Clear Outputs
content.value_statement: Agile teams often lose decisions, blockers, and follow-up actions across meetings. This skill provides structured formats for standups, retrospectives, sprint progress updates, and weekly summaries.
actual_capabilities: Structures individual, team, and async daily standup updates.
Creates sprint progress summaries with burndown, story status, risks, and decisions.
Facilitates retrospectives with wins, issues, learnings, team health, and action items.
Documents owners, due dates, blockers, dependencies, and follow-up actions.
Builds weekly summaries with accomplishments, metrics, learnings, and next-week focus.
limitations: Does not connect to Jira, GitHub, Slack, or other external systems.
Does not calculate real metrics unless the user provides source data.
Does not replace a trained scrum master for complex team conflict.
Uses generic templates that may need adaptation for each team.

use_cases.title: Prepare a daily team standup
use_cases.description: Convert scattered updates into a concise standup agenda with blockers and action items.

use_cases.title: Summarize sprint progress
use_cases.description: Create a clear sprint status report with completed work, remaining risks, and decisions needed.

use_cases.title: Run a healthier retrospective
use_cases.description: Capture team feedback, quantify team health, and turn discussion into owned improvement actions.

prompt_templates.title: Create a standup update
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a daily standup update for this team member. Include yesterday, today, blockers, and any needed help: [paste notes].

prompt_templates.title: Build a team standup agenda
prompt_templates.prompt: Turn these team updates into a 15-minute standup agenda. Highlight completed work, active work, blockers, owners, and follow-up actions: [paste updates].

prompt_templates.title: Facilitate a sprint retrospective
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare a sprint retrospective from these notes. Include what went well, what did not go well, learnings, team health, and action items: [paste notes].

prompt_templates.title: Create an executive sprint summary
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a weekly sprint summary for stakeholders. Include progress metrics, shipped work, risks, decisions needed, learnings, and next-week focus: [paste sprint data].

output_examples.input: Three developers provided updates with one dependency blocker and two completed stories.
output_examples.output: A standup summary with completed work, planned work, a blocker owner, a due date, and a short notes section.

output_examples.input: A sprint ended with mixed velocity, several bugs fixed, and unresolved review delays.
output_examples.output: A retrospective agenda that separates wins, issues, root causes, learnings, team health scores, and improvement actions.

output_examples.input: Stakeholders need a concise update on sprint progress and release risk.
output_examples.output: A weekly summary covering shipped features, in-progress work, metric trends, risks, decisions, and next-week priorities.

best_practices: Provide current sprint goals, team updates, blockers, and known dependencies before asking for ceremony output.
Ask for owners and due dates on every action item.
Review previous retrospective actions before creating new improvement items.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill to invent sprint metrics without source data.
Do not turn standups into long status meetings without clear blockers and decisions.
Do not capture retrospective complaints without converting them into specific actions.

faq.question: Can this skill run async standups?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes an async standup format suitable for chat tools when updates are provided by team members.

faq.question: Does it integrate with project management tools?
faq.answer: No. It does not connect to external tools. Paste relevant sprint data into the prompt.

faq.question: Can it help with sprint planning?
faq.answer: Yes. It can organize goals, risks, dependencies, and decisions for planning discussions.

faq.question: Can it calculate velocity?
faq.answer: Only when planned and actual story point data is provided. It does not fetch metrics automatically.

faq.question: Is it only for Scrum teams?
faq.answer: No. The templates work best for Scrum, but teams can adapt them for general agile workflows.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide sprint dates, goals, team updates, completed work, planned work, blockers, metrics, and previous action items.
